title: Share of the population who don't drink alcohol, United Kingdom
subtitle: Share of the total population of a given demographic who don't drink alcohol.
caption:
Share of the population who don't drink alcohol, United Kingdom

Share of the total population of a given demographic who don't drink alcohol.

This horizontal bar chart displays the share of the population in the United Kingdom who do not drink alcohol in 2016, broken down by gender and age group. The data is sourced from the Office for National Statistics (ONS). The bars are color-coded, with teal representing female demographics and orange representing male demographics. The highest share of non-drinkers is found among females aged 65 and over, at 32.9%. This is followed by females aged 16 to 24 at 27.7% and males in the same 16 to 24 age bracket at 25.9%. The aggregate category for all females aged 16 and over stands at 24.4%, while females aged 25 to 44 account for 23.1%. In the middle of the distribution, females aged 45 to 64 represent 18% of non-drinkers. The male demographics generally show lower shares in the older age brackets compared to their female counterparts. Males aged 16 and over collectively sit at 17.3%, closely followed by males aged 25 to 44 at 17.1%. The lowest shares on the chart are observed in the older male groups, with males aged 65 and over at 15.9% and males aged 45 to 64 recording the lowest value of 14.4%. The visual arrangement highlights a distinct pattern where the oldest female group has the highest abstention rate, while the middle-aged male groups have the lowest.
